title: cracca oblongifolia
intro: Cracca oblongifolia, also known as the kidney vetch, is a perennial plant species belonging to the pea family, Fabaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and diuretic effects, making it beneficial for treating urinary tract issues and kidney problems. The plant's leaves and flowers have been used to prepare herbal remedies, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can help to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and promote overall health. In addition to its medicinal properties, Cracca oblongifolia has been used to treat a range of health issues, including digestive problems, skin conditions, and respiratory issues.
